description_long: This is a traditional stringed musical instrument with a distinctive triangular wooden body, most closely resembling a Russian balalaika. The body is made of light-colored wood with a black pickguard area and features two visible sound holes near the bridge. The neck is long and slender, with a dark fingerboard and several frets, leading to a headstock with three tuning pegs arranged in a straight line. The bridge and tailpiece are simple, and the instrument has three strings stretched from the tailpiece to the headstock. The overall craftsmanship suggests a hand-made or folk design, with visible wood grain and a matte finish.
description: A triangular-bodied, wooden stringed instrument with a long neck, three strings, and a simple headstock, resembling a balalaika.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the triangular body, neck, and headstock, highlighting the instrument's slim profile.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the wide triangular face, black pickguard, sound holes, bridge, and string arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the triangular body and neck, with the pickguard and bridge visible from a different angle.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the headstock, neck, and the upper part of the triangular body, with tuning pegs clearly visible.
attribute_headstock_design: Rectangular headstock with three inline tuning pegs, no visible logo, simple and unadorned.
